evidence: query/ package (5 files, 1806 LOC) hosts both query orchestration (BlackLabQueryExecutor, QueryExecutor) and a large result-DTO namespace (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions) as inner classes of SemanticFieldExplorer — concerns that belong in separate packages share one file

utils/ package (4 files) is a heterogeneous catch-all: CqlUtils (CQL parsing), LogDiceCalculator (statistical scoring), LongIntHashMap (general data structure), PosGroup (domain enum) — these have no common theme beyond 'not fitting elsewhere'

api/ package (3 files, 1167 LOC) is the largest per-file average; PatternSubstitution is a static utility that could live in utils/ given it depends on CqlUtils — its placement in api/ is an import-affinity mismatch (its primary import is utils.CqlUtils, not other api/ classes)
config/ package (2 files: GrammarConfigLoader + RelationType) is appropriately cohesive
No package has more than 10 files; flat directory overload is not an issue at the current scale

strengths: Top-level package split (api/, config/, query/, utils/) maps cleanly to architectural layers; a new developer can orient quickly
config/ package is coherent: GrammarConfigLoader and RelationType are co-located and have no leakage into other domains
No directory has more than 5 files, so navigation within packages is fast
package_organization.issue_character: The organization issues are localized to two packages: query/ hosts a large DTO namespace as inner classes that should be a separate model/ package, and utils/ is a shallow catch-all that mixes CQL parsing, statistics, data structures, and a domain enum.
package_organization.score_rationale: The package structure is mostly coherent and a developer can find their way around. The main friction is that query/ conflates query execution with result modeling (forcing 6 inner DTO imports into WordSketchApiServer), and utils/ has no organizing principle beyond 'miscellaneous'. These are navigable problems rather than misleading ones — a developer knows to look in query/ for SEF results even if that is an odd home — which puts the score in the 70-75 range rather than the 60-70 'must search' range.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer hosts 7 result DTO inner classes that inflate query/ and force api/ to import query internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports 6 inner classes from SemanticFieldExplorer: ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions
SemanticFieldExplorer.java is 650+ LOC; a significant portion is DTO record/class definitions rather than query logic
issues.suggestion: Stage 1: Create src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/model/ package. Stage 2: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions into separate files in model/. Stage 3: Update imports in SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer (run `mvn compile` to validate no broken references). Stage 4: Remove the @see note in SemanticFieldExplorer javadoc once done.

issues.summary: utils/ packages four unrelated concerns (CQL parsing, statistics, data structures, domain enum) with no organizing theme
evidence: CqlUtils parses CQL token syntax; LogDiceCalculator computes association scores; LongIntHashMap is a generic off-heap map; PosGroup is a domain enum for POS grouping — no shared concern across these four files
PatternSubstitution in api/ depends primarily on CqlUtils, suggesting CqlUtils has stronger affinity with pattern-processing utilities than with the api/ layer where PatternSubstitution lives
issues.suggestion: Consider splitting utils/ into: scoring/ (LogDiceCalculator), collections/ or keeping LongIntHashMap in utils/ as a genuine general utility, and moving PosGroup into config/ (it is a domain concept used by GrammarConfigLoader). CqlUtils could move to a grammar/ or parsing/ sub-package alongside PatternSubstitution. Run `mvn compile` after each move to catch import breaks.

root_causes: SEF result DTOs were added as inner classes during initial development before a model/ layer was established; later growth made the coupling visible
utils/ accumulated files organically without a naming policy; PosGroup in particular is a domain concept that slipped into a generic utilities package
